commitMessage: fix: allow machine key auth on PATCH /projects/{id}

content.summary: Stores and serves AI-generated feature ideas in Azure Table Storage. Accepts browser JWT tokens via EasyAuth for read access and an X-Ideas-Key machine write-key for automated writes. Exposes endpoints to list and create ideas, manage projects, and trigger the ideas-bot Container App Job.
content.description: Azure Functions app that stores and serves feature ideas in Table Storage. Supports two auth modes: browser JWT tokens via EasyAuth for read access, and an X-Ideas-Key machine write-key for automated writes. Exposes a trigger endpoint for the ideas-bot.
features: List and create ideas in Azure Table Storage
List and create projects — ideas are grouped under projects
List, create, and delete per-idea status updates (comments section)
EasyAuth JWT authentication for browser access (read)
X-Ideas-Key machine write-key for automated idea creation (write)
POST /api/ideas/{id}/run-bot — triggers the ideas-bot Container App Job for a specific idea, with optional model override
PATCH /api/projects/{id} — updates project metadata, including associated repositories
Projects now support an optional 'repos' field (list of repository URLs)
Ideas, projects, and updates stored in separate Table Storage tables

architecture.overview: HTTP-triggered Azure Functions backed by Table Storage. EasyAuth handles browser auth transparently at the Azure layer — no auth code in the application. A separate machine key enables automation (ideas-bot, external scripts) to write ideas without user credentials.
keyPoints: EasyAuth injects the validated Google ID token as a request header — the function reads sub and email from it
X-Ideas-Key is a long-lived secret stored as a Function App setting (not in the codebase)
run-bot endpoint starts a Container App Job execution via the Azure SDK and returns immediately, with optional model override passed as AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT
PATCH /projects/{id} endpoint updates project metadata, including the 'repos' field stored as a JSON array in Table Storage
No compute runs between requests — fully serverless, scales to zero
Table Storage partition key = project ID; row key = idea ID (UUID)
Updates table uses idea ID as the partition key and UUID as the row key
Projects table uses a fixed partition key ('projects') and UUID as the row key

dataFlow.label: User updates project metadata
sublines: updates 'repos' field in the projects table

designDocs.title: ADR: Per-Idea Status Updates
designDocs.description: Adds a threaded updates log to each idea, backed by a new Azure Table Storage table and a detail slide-over panel in the frontend.
